title: Utilities for Working with Age Categories
text: ages_to_interval() provides categorisation ages based specified right-hand interval limits. resultant groupings span natural numbers (0) always closed left open right. example, limits = c(1,10,30) possible groupings “[0, 1)”, “[1, 10)”, “[10, 30)” “[30, Inf)”. roughly comparable call cut(ages, right = FALSE, breaks = c(0, limits)) start end points interval returned entries list. Note default limits argument functions set limits = c(1L, 5L, 15L, 25L, 45L, 65L) clarity examples explicitly set value used.

title: Utilities for Working with Age Categories
text: split_interval_counts() splits counts within age interval counts individuals years based given weighting. Age intervals specified lower (closed) upper (open) bounds, .e. intervals form [lower, upper).
code: # by default counts are split equally across ages within intervals split_interval_counts(     lower_bounds = c(0L, 5L, 10L),     upper_bounds = c(5L, 10L, 20L),     counts = c(5L, 10L, 30L) ) #>    age count #> 1    0     1 #> 2    1     1 #> 3    2     1 #> 4    3     1 #> 5    4     1 #> 6    5     2 #> 7    6     2 #> 8    7     2 #> 9    8     2 #> 10   9     2 #> 11  10     3 #> 12  11     3 #> 13  12     3 #> 14  13     3 #> 15  14     3 #> 16  15     3 #> 17  16     3 #> 18  17     3 #> 19  18     3 #> 20  19     3  # Population weightings to apply for individual years can be specified by # the weights argument. If these are specified, they must be of length # `max_upper` and represent weights in the range 0:(max_upper - 1). max_upper <- 20L weights <- integer(max_upper) weights[c(TRUE, FALSE)] <- 1L split_interval_counts(     lower_bounds = c(0L, 5L, 10L),     upper_bounds = c(5L, 10L, 20L),     counts = c(5L, 10L, 30L),     max_upper = max_upper,     weights <- weights ) #>    age    count #> 1    0 1.666667 #> 2    1 0.000000 #> 3    2 1.666667 #> 4    3 0.000000 #> 5    4 1.666667 #> 6    5 0.000000 #> 7    6 5.000000 #> 8    7 0.000000 #> 9    8 5.000000 #> 10   9 0.000000 #> 11  10 6.000000 #> 12  11 0.000000 #> 13  12 6.000000 #> 14  13 0.000000 #> 15  14 6.000000 #> 16  15 0.000000 #> 17  16 6.000000 #> 18  17 0.000000 #> 19  18 6.000000 #> 20  19 0.000000

title: Utilities for Working with Age Categories
text: aggregate_age_counts() provides aggregation counts across ages (years). similar cut() tapply() pattern optimised speed flexibility. Groupings ages_to_interval() counts provided across natural numbers well missing values.
code: # default ages generated as 0:(length(counts) - 1L) if only counts provided. aggregate_age_counts(counts = 1:65, limits = c(1L, 5L, 15L, 25L, 45L, 65L)) #>    interval lower_bound upper_bound count #> 1    [0, 1)           0           1     1 #> 2    [1, 5)           1           5    14 #> 3   [5, 15)           5          15   105 #> 4  [15, 25)          15          25   205 #> 5  [25, 45)          25          45   710 #> 6  [45, 65)          45          65  1110 #> 7 [65, Inf)          65         Inf     0 #> 8      <NA>          NA          NA     0  aggregate_age_counts(counts = 1:65, limits = 50) #>    interval lower_bound upper_bound count #> 1   [0, 50)           0          50  1275 #> 2 [50, Inf)          50         Inf   870 #> 3      <NA>          NA          NA     0  # NA ages are handled with their own grouping ages <- 1:65 ages[1:44] <- NA aggregate_age_counts(     counts = 1:65,     ages = ages,     limits = c(1L, 5L, 15L, 25L, 45L, 65L) ) #>    interval lower_bound upper_bound count #> 1    [0, 1)           0           1     0 #> 2    [1, 5)           1           5     0 #> 3   [5, 15)           5          15     0 #> 4  [15, 25)          15          25     0 #> 5  [25, 45)          25          45     0 #> 6  [45, 65)          45          65  1090 #> 7 [65, Inf)          65         Inf    65 #> 8      <NA>          NA          NA   990

title: Utilities for Working with Age Categories
text: reaggregate_interval_counts() equivalent , efficient , calling split_interval_counts() aggregate_age_counts(). example shows can used redistribute counts across desired set age intervals. use data included package obtained 2021 census modify based desired interval limits.
code: # census data data(pop_dat) pop_dat #>    area_code         area_name age_category   value #> 1  K04000001 England and Wales       [0, 5) 3232100 #> 2  K04000001 England and Wales      [5, 10) 3524600 #> 3  K04000001 England and Wales     [10, 15) 3595900 #> 4  K04000001 England and Wales     [15, 20) 3394700 #> 5  K04000001 England and Wales     [20, 25) 3602100 #> 6  K04000001 England and Wales     [25, 30) 3901800 #> 7  K04000001 England and Wales     [30, 35) 4148800 #> 8  K04000001 England and Wales     [35, 40) 3981600 #> 9  K04000001 England and Wales     [40, 45) 3755700 #> 10 K04000001 England and Wales     [45, 50) 3788700 #> 11 K04000001 England and Wales     [50, 55) 4123400 #> 12 K04000001 England and Wales     [55, 60) 4029000 #> 13 K04000001 England and Wales     [60, 65) 3455700 #> 14 K04000001 England and Wales     [65, 70) 2945100 #> 15 K04000001 England and Wales     [70, 75) 2978000 #> 16 K04000001 England and Wales     [75, 80) 2170300 #> 17 K04000001 England and Wales     [80, 85) 1517000 #> 18 K04000001 England and Wales     [85, 90)  925100 #> 19 K04000001 England and Wales    [90, Inf)  527900  # each row is for the same region so discard for moment dat <- subset(pop_dat, select = c(age_category, value))  # extract upper and lower bounds dat <- transform(     dat,     lower_bound = as.numeric(sub("\\[([0-9]+), .+)", "\\1", age_category)),     upper_bound = as.numeric(sub(".+, (.+))", "\\1", age_category)) )  head(dat, n=10) #>    age_category   value lower_bound upper_bound #> 1        [0, 5) 3232100           0           5 #> 2       [5, 10) 3524600           5          10 #> 3      [10, 15) 3595900          10          15 #> 4      [15, 20) 3394700          15          20 #> 5      [20, 25) 3602100          20          25 #> 6      [25, 30) 3901800          25          30 #> 7      [30, 35) 4148800          30          35 #> 8      [35, 40) 3981600          35          40 #> 9      [40, 45) 3755700          40          45 #> 10     [45, 50) 3788700          45          50  # recategorise based on ages with(     dat,     reaggregate_interval_counts(         lower_bounds = lower_bound,         upper_bounds = upper_bound,         counts = value,         limits = c(1L, 5L, 15L, 25L, 45L, 65L),         max_upper = 100L,         weights = NULL     ) ) #>    interval lower_bound upper_bound    count #> 1    [0, 1)           0           1   646420 #> 2    [1, 5)           1           5  2585680 #> 3   [5, 15)           5          15  7120500 #> 4  [15, 25)          15          25  6996800 #> 5  [25, 45)          25          45 15787900 #> 6  [45, 65)          45          65 15396800 #> 7 [65, Inf)          65         Inf 11063400 #> 8      <NA>          NA          NA        0

code: @Manual{,   title = {ympes: Collection of Helper Functions},   author = {Tim Taylor},   year = {2022},   note = {R package version 0.2.1.9000},   url = {https://timtaylor.github.io/ympes/}, }

title: Collection of Helper Functions
text: ympes provides collection lightweight helper functions (imps) interactive use inclusion within packages. include functions visualising colour palettes, quoting user input, working age intervals set minimal assertion functions.  can install released version ympes CRAN : development version ympes can installed GitHub : Image Le grand Etteilla, ou, l’art de tirer les cartes. Author Simon Blocquel (1780–1863, pseudonym Julia Orsini). B+W version (details) obtained wikipedia.

title: Utilities for Age Intervals — ageutils
text: help page documents utility functions provided working age intervals: ages_to_interval() provides categorisation ages based specified right-hand interval limits. resultant groupings span natural numbers (0) always closed left open right. example, limits = c(1,10,30) possible groupings "[0, 1)", "[1, 10)", "[10, 30)" "[30, Inf)". roughly comparable call cut(ages, right = FALSE, breaks = c(0, limits)) start end points interval returned entries list. split_interval_counts() splits counts within age interval counts individuals years based given weighting. Age intervals specified lower (closed) upper (open) bounds, .e. intervals form [lower, upper). aggregate_age_counts() provides aggregation counts across ages (years). similar cut() tapply() pattern optimised speed flexibility. Groupings ages_to_interval() counts provided across natural numbers well missing values. reaggregate_interval_counts() equivalent , efficient , calling split_interval_counts() aggregate_age_counts().

title: Utilities for Age Intervals — ageutils
code: ages_to_interval(ages, limits = c(1L, 5L, 15L, 25L, 45L, 65L))  split_interval_counts(   lower_bounds,   upper_bounds,   counts,   max_upper = 100L,   weights = NULL )  aggregate_age_counts(   counts,   ages = 0:(length(counts) - 1L),   limits = c(1L, 5L, 15L, 25L, 45L, 65L) )  reaggregate_interval_counts(   lower_bounds,   upper_bounds,   counts,   limits = c(1L, 5L, 15L, 25L, 45L, 65L),   max_upper = 100L,   weights = NULL )

title: Utilities for Age Intervals — ageutils
text: ages [integerish]. Vector age years. Double values coerced integer prior categorisation / aggregation. aggregate_age_counts(), must corresponding counts entry defaults 0:(N-1) N number counts present. ages >= 200 permitted due internal implementation. limits [integerish]. 1 positive cut points increasing (strictly) order. Defaults c(1L,5L,15L,25L,45L,65L). Double values coerced integer prior categorisation. lower_bounds, upper_bounds [integerish]. pair vectors representing bounds intervals. lower_bounds must strictly less upper_bounds greater equal zero. Missing (NA) bounds permitted. Double vectors coerced integer. counts [numeric]. Vector counts aggregated. max_upper [integerish] Represents maximum upper bounds permitted upon splitting data. Used replace Inf upper bounds prior splitting. upper_bound greater max_upper function error. Double vectors coerced integer. weights [numeric] Population weightings apply individual years. NULL (default) counts split evenly based interval size. specified, must length max_upper represent weights range 0:(max_upper - 1).

title: Utilities for Age Intervals — ageutils
text: ages_to_interval(). data frame ordered factor column (interval), well columns corresponding explicit bounds (lower_bound upper_bound). split_interval_counts(). data frame entries age (years) count. aggregate_age_counts() reaggregate_interval_counts(). data frame 4 entries; interval, lower_bound, upper_bound associated count.

title: Argument assertions — assertions
text: Assertions function arguments. Motivated vctrs::vec_assert() lower overhead cost less informative error messages. Designed make easy identify top level calling function whether used within user facing function internally.

title: Argument assertions — assertions
code: assert_integer(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_int(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_double(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_dbl(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_numeric(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_num(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_logical(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_lgl(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_character(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_chr(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_data_frame(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_list(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_integer(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_int(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_double(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_dbl(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_numeric(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_num(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_logical(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_lgl(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_bool(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_boolean(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_character(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_scalar_chr(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))  assert_string(x, arg = deparse(substitute(x)), call = sys.call(-1L))

title: Argument assertions — assertions
text: x Argument check. arg Name argument checked (used error message). call Call use error message.

title: Argument assertions — assertions
text: NULL assertion succeeds (error otherwise).

title: Argument assertions — assertions
code: # Use in a user facing function fun <- function(i, d, l, chr, b) {     assert_scalar_int(i)     TRUE } fun(i=1L) #> [1] TRUE try(fun()) #> Error in fun() : argument `i` is missing, with no default. try(fun(i="cat")) #> Error in fun() : `i` must be an integer vector of length 1.  # Use in an internal function internal_fun <- function(a) {     assert_string(a, arg = deparse(substitute(a)), call = sys.call(-1L))     TRUE } external_fun <- function(b) {     internal_fun(a=b) } external_fun(b="cat") #> [1] TRUE try(external_fun()) #> Error in external_fun() : argument `b` is missing, with no default. try(external_fun(a = letters)) #> Error in external_fun(a = letters) : unused argument (a = letters)

title: Quote names — cc
text: cc() quotes comma separated names whilst trimming outer whitespace. intended interactive use .

title: Quote names — cc
text: ... Unquoted names (separated commas) wish quote; empty arguments (e.g. third item one,two,,four) returned "". .clip code generate constructed character vector copied system clipboard; defaults FALSE unless option "imp.clipboard" set TRUE.

title: Quote names — cc
text: character vector quoted input.

title: Quote names — cc
text: Copying clipboard requires availability package clipr.

title: Plot a colour palette — plot_palette
text: plot_palette() plots palette vector colour values (name hex).

title: Plot a colour palette — plot_palette
text: values character vector named hex colours. label boolean. want label plot ? values named vector names used labels, otherwise, values. square boolean. Display palette square?

title: Plot a colour palette — plot_palette
text: input (invisibly).

title: Aggregated population data — pop_dat

what: ympes: Collection of Helper Functions — ympes-package
title: ympes: Collection of Helper Functions — ympes-package
text: Provides collection lightweight helper functions (imps) interactive use inclusion within packages. include minimal assertion functions focus informative error messaging missing incorrect function arguments well functions visualising colour palettes, quoting user input working age intervals.

title: ympes: Collection of Helper Functions — ympes-package
text: Maintainer: Tim Taylor tim.taylor@hiddenelephants.co.uk (ORCID) [copyright holder]

title: ympes (development version)
text: New assertion functions require imp_ prefix return NULL success. Note returning NULL means new assertions pipe friendly implementation slightly efficient. old format assertions (imp_ prefix; e.g. imp_assert_int()) now deprecated removed future release.

text: CRAN release: 2022-10-26

title: ympes 0.2.0
text: New functions working age intervals: ages_to_interval() provides categorisation ages based specified right-hand interval limits. split_interval_counts() splits counts within age interval counts individuals years based given weighting. aggregate_age_counts() provides aggregation counts across ages (years). reaggregate_interval_counts() equivalent calling split_interval_counts() followed aggregate_age_counts().

title: ympes 0.2.0
text: testing framework used ympes migrated testhat tinytest.

title: ympes 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2022-08-09 Initial release ympes provides collection lightweight helper functions (imps) interactive use inclusion within packages.

title: ympes 0.1.1
text: assertions integer, double, numeric, logical, bool, character (string), list data frame values imp_assert_integer(), imp_assert_int() imp_assert_double() , imp_assert_dbl() imp_assert_numeric(), imp_assert_num() imp_assert_logical(), imp_assert_lgl() imp_assert_character(), imp_assert_chr() imp_assert_list() imp_assert_data_frame() imp_assert_scalar_integer(), imp_assert_scalar_int() imp_assert_scalar_double(), imp_assert_scalar_dbl() imp_assert_scalar_numeric(), imp_assert_scalar_num() imp_assert_scalar_logical(), imp_assert_scalar_lgl() imp_assert_boolean(), imp_assert_bool() imp_assert_scalar_character(), imp_assert_scalar_chr(), imp_assert_string() cc() interactive quoting comma separated names. plot_palette() visualising colour palettes.
